task: Add the next Pro Rust product-core slice: a keyless pre-execution boundary for hosted auth, credential-vault handles, and quota reservation prerequisites. Expose one local API endpoint, render one graph-first web panel, and synchronize Pro docs/evidence. This remains preview-only and must not add sessions, passwords, JWT validation, external auth, credential storage, provider calls, quota or billing mutation, worker execution, package publishing, npm dependencies, or OSS runtime changes.
securityRequirements: Mention auth, secrets, permissions, and sensitive-data handling explicitly.
Do not accept or store passwords, tokens, provider secrets, or API keys.
Pre-execution boundary must deny live/provider/worker/result-write paths until hosted auth, vault-handle, and quota-reservation gates exist.
dependencyRequirements: Mention new or upgraded packages, lockfile changes, and dependency impact explicitly.
performanceRequirements: Mention performance, latency, or load impact in evidence.
understandingRequirements: Explain why this boundary composes existing hosted auth, vault, quota, worker, and commit vocabulary instead of enabling execution.
continuityRequirements: Reuse the existing workspace access-gate, credential-vault, quota-ledger, Axum endpoint style, and compact web panel patterns.
patternSummary: Reuse the existing Pro domain boundary payloads, API route handlers, web fetchJson helpers, and preview-only boundary panels.
smallestViableChange: Add a deterministic pre-execution payload that reports live execution denied until hosted auth, credential-vault handle, quota reservation, worker lease, and idempotent commit gates are satisfied.
assumptions: The Pro runtime remains local preview-only.
Real provider/search execution is still intentionally blocked.
Credential values are never accepted, displayed, stored, or returned by this boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: Domain tests prove the pre-execution boundary denies live provider calls and marks hosted auth, vault-handle, quota-reservation, worker-lease, and idempotent-commit prerequisites as blocking.
API tests prove the endpoint returns a server-computed pre-execution payload and no secret-shaped fields.
Web tests prove the graph-first shell contains the pre-execution panel, endpoint path, and renderer.
Docs and evidence explain that this is not real provider execution, credential access, quota mutation, or auth enforcement.
expectedBehaviorChanges: Pro API exposes a keyless pre-execution boundary endpoint.
Pro web shell renders whether live execution is still denied and which hosted prerequisites block it.
riskJustification: This is a keyless local pre-execution evaluator that composes existing denied hosted gates. It prepares an explicit execution boundary without enabling provider calls, credential reads, quota reservations, worker execution, or result writes.
